implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-002: Supervisor exposes the AsyncSubAgentMiddleware tool surface
AC-003: Supervisor stays responsive while autobuild is in flight
AC-004: build_supervisor wires emitter + 4 wave-2 collaborators without constructing a second emitter
AC-005: dispatch_autobuild_async has exactly the five required collaborator parameters
AC-006: Modified files pass ruff lint cleanly

completion_promises.criterion_text: dispatch_autobuild_async accepts lifecycle_emitter and threads it into the start_async_task context payload.
completion_promises.evidence: Added `lifecycle_emitter: PipelineLifecycleEmitter | None = None` to `dispatch_autobuild_async`'s keyword-only parameter list (src/forge/pipeline/dispatchers/autobuild_async.py:304). Threaded it into the `launch_payload` passed to `start_async_task` as `ctx['lifecycle_emitter']` (line 466). DDR-007 §Decision Option A is honoured. Test `TestEmitterThreadedIntoContext::test_dispatch_threads_emitter_into_ctx` verifies `ctx['lifecycle_emitter'] is fake_emitter` by object identity.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The supervisor's tool list includes the AsyncSubAgentMiddleware's start/check/update/cancel/list tools.
completion_promises.evidence: Added `tools: tuple[Any, ...] = field(default_factory=tuple)` field to `Supervisor` dataclass (src/forge/pipeline/supervisor.py). Added `_build_async_subagent_middleware()` helper in src/forge/cli/serve.py that constructs `AsyncSubAgentMiddleware` with `graph_id=AUTOBUILD_RUNNER_NAME`. `build_supervisor()` populates `Supervisor.tools` from `middleware.tools`. Test `TestAsyncSubAgentToolListExposed::test_default_middleware_factory_emits_five_tools` confirms the five canonical tool names {start_async_task, check_async_task, update_async_task, cancel_async_task, list_async_tasks} are present.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The supervisor stays responsive (answers status queries) while autobuild's async task is in flight (Group A scenario).
completion_promises.evidence: The `_make_autobuild_dispatcher_closure` returns a synchronous callable that delegates to `dispatch_autobuild_async` — `start_async_task` returns immediately with a task_id by ADR-ARCH-031 contract. Test `TestSupervisorRemainsResponsive::test_next_turn_returns_waiting_while_autobuild_in_flight` dispatches autobuild then runs `await sup.next_turn('build-2')` under `asyncio.wait_for(timeout=2.0)` and asserts the turn completes (outcome WAITING/NO_OP) without blocking on the in-flight task. Test `test_supervisor_dispatcher_closure_is_synchronous` asserts the closure result is not a coroutine.

completion_promises.criterion_text: _run_serve constructs the supervisor with the emitter + the four Wave 2 collaborators; no second emitter is constructed.
completion_promises.evidence: Added `build_supervisor()` factory in src/forge/cli/serve.py that accepts `lifecycle_emitter` as a parameter and threads it into BOTH the `Supervisor.lifecycle_emitter` field AND the autobuild dispatcher closure (via `_make_autobuild_dispatcher_closure`). The factory does not call `PipelineLifecycleEmitter(...)` itself — the caller-supplied instance is the only emitter. Test `TestBuildSupervisorWiring::test_supervisor_receives_lifecycle_emitter` asserts `sup.lifecycle_emitter is fake_emitter` by identity. Test `test_factory_does_not_construct_a_second_emitter` recovers the bound emitter via `dispatcher.__wrapped_emitter__` and asserts identity. Note: actual call from `_run_serve` is gated on TASK-FW10-007's `_serve_deps.py` factory which is not yet wired; the factory is exposed and tested independently.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Diff inspection: dispatch_autobuild_async has exactly five collaborator parameters (forward_context_builder, async_task_starter, stage_log_recorder, state_channel, lifecycle_emitter).
completion_promises.evidence: Test `TestDispatchAutobuildSignature::test_signature_has_exactly_five_collaborator_kwargs` extracts all keyword-only parameters via `inspect.signature` and asserts equality with the exact ordered list ['forward_context_builder', 'async_task_starter', 'stage_log_recorder', 'state_channel', 'lifecycle_emitter']. Verified via inspection: `python3 -c "import inspect; from forge.pipeline.dispatchers.autobuild_async import dispatch_autobuild_async; print([n for n,p in inspect.signature(dispatch_autobuild_async).parameters.items() if p.kind is inspect.Parameter.KEYWORD_ONLY])"` returns the five expected names.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: `python3 -m ruff check src/forge/cli/serve.py src/forge/pipeline/dispatchers/autobuild_async.py tests/forge/test_supervisor_async_subagent_wiring.py` reports `All checks passed!`. The 3 pre-existing F821 'Undefined name Build' errors in src/forge/pipeline/supervisor.py are NOT introduced by this task — they exist in the baseline (verified by `git stash && ruff check` reproducing them on the unmodified file). My addition to supervisor.py (tools / lifecycle_emitter fields) does not introduce new lint errors.
